subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the poem’s narrative structure and legend-based storytelling
Interpret moral, symbolic, and cultural elements in the poem
Examine character traits and their significance
Develop critical thinking and literary analysis skills
Understand the use of rhyme, rhythm, and repetition in folklore poetry

lesson_plan.title: Introduction and Context
topics_covered: Background of Northland legends and folklore
Poet’s style in narrative and moral poems
Theme: selfishness, consequences, and moral lessons
teaching_strategy: Lecture on folklore and legend traditions
Discussion on why legends are used to teach morals
student_activities: Discuss other examples of moral stories or legends

lesson_plan.title: Narrative Analysis – Story Flow
topics_covered: Examine the actions of the little woman and Saint Peter
teaching_strategy: Explain chronological events and plot twists
Encourage students to map story events visually
student_activities: Create a flowchart of story events
Annotate the text with causes and consequences

lesson_plan.title: Character Analysis and Symbolism
topics_covered: Character traits of the little woman and Saint Peter
Symbolism of transformation into a woodpecker
teaching_strategy: Ask students to justify character actions
student_activities: Explain symbolic meaning of the transformation in own words

lesson_plan.title: Poetic Devices and Rhyme Scheme
topics_covered: Rhyme, rhythm, repetition, and narrative techniques
How devices enhance storytelling and mood
teaching_strategy: Discuss effect of rhyme and rhythm on the story’s flow
student_activities: Write examples of imagery and descriptive words

lesson_plan.title: Moral, Ethical Reflection, and Cultural Context
topics_covered: Analyze the moral of the poem
Discuss ethical lessons about selfishness and consequences
teaching_strategy: Class debate on moral and ethical implications
Guide discussion on relevance to modern life
student_activities: Write reflective paragraph on moral lessons

lesson_plan.title: Revision, Analytical Writing, and Assessment
topics_covered: Recap story events, characters, and moral lessons
Short analytical and reflective writing on poem
Discussion on cultural significance of folklore poetry
teaching_strategy: Quick recap of key points and devices
Guide students to write analytical summary
student_activities: Write analytical essay connecting story, characters, and moral

teaching_aids: Charts highlighting characters, plot, and moral lessons
Handouts with reflection and analysis questions
teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyze the legend critically, interpret symbolic elements, connect the moral to their own experiences, and explore the use of poetic devices in narrative storytelling.
